evals.name: workflow-implied-entity
evals.prompt: Run sdlc-data against the docs/PRD.yaml and docs/UX.yaml in this project. For every multiple-choice question, pick the recommended (position-1) option. For free-text questions, give a one-line plausible answer. Skip optional themes during the gate. The PRD declares WKF-001..WKF-004 and a single ENT-001 (Project) — but WKF-003 ('switch git branch and continue work on a separate isolated stream') strongly implies a per-branch session-style entity that NO FR-NNN and NO ENT-NNN names directly. Verify that the agent's scope-completeness sweep at the end of the entities theme catches this and proposes a BranchSession-style entity. Then write DATA-MODEL.yaml and validate.
evals.expected_output: A docs/DATA-MODEL.yaml with at least 2 entities — the PRD's Project plus the sweep-derived BranchSession-style entity. Every entity has traces_prd_features in FR-NNN format (validator-enforced), traces_ux_surfaces in SCR-NNN format (validator-enforced), and the sweep-derived entity has traces_prd_workflows containing 'WKF-003'. metadata.changelog is present and prepended with at least one entry of the form '<ver> (<YYYY-MM-DD>): ...'. All data_warnings entries (if any) start with WRN-NNN. validate_schema.py exits 0 and emits [OK] with status complete.

evals.name: existing-data-update-flow
evals.prompt: Run sdlc-data against the existing docs/PRD.yaml, docs/UX.yaml, and docs/DATA-MODEL.yaml in this project. The DATA-MODEL.yaml is stale: it predates the v1.1 conventions (no metadata.changelog), has no WRN-NNN warnings, only traces FR-001/FR-002, and is missing the Comment entity that FR-003 / WKF-003 imply. For every choice, pick the recommended (position-1) option; for free-text, give a one-line plausible answer; accept any sweep candidate the agent proposes. Then write DATA-MODEL.yaml and validate.
evals.expected_output: The on-disk DATA-MODEL.yaml is rewritten with: (a) a new Comment entity whose traces_prd_features contains 'FR-003' and traces_ux_surfaces contains 'SCR-003'; (b) metadata.changelog populated with at least ONE new entry prepended (most-recent first); (c) every data_warnings entry — if any — formatted as 'WRN-NNN: ...'; (d) the existing Task entity's traces preserved; (e) metadata.data_model_version bumped to 1.1 (or higher). validate_schema.py exits 0 with [OK] and status complete.

evals.name: file-native-paradigm
evals.prompt: Run sdlc-data against the docs/PRD.yaml and docs/UX.yaml in this project. This is a single-user CLI ('outline') whose PRD storage_preferences explicitly want serialized YAML files on the filesystem, no database server, git-friendly and hand-editable, with kilobytes of data. At the Phase 4 paradigm-selection step the agent should recommend the file_native paradigm (Pydantic models serialized to disk). For every multiple-choice question pick the recommended (position-1) option; for free-text give a one-line plausible answer; skip optional themes at the gate; accept any sweep candidate. Then write DATA-MODEL.yaml and validate.
evals.expected_output: A docs/DATA-MODEL.yaml with persistence.paradigm == 'file_native' and persistence.primary_store == 'filesystem'. It has a non-empty identity_conventions.rules list (NOT an id_strategy.scheme), entity fields use pydantic_type (not a SQL type enum), and the relational-only blocks (relationships, indexes_and_queries, integrity_and_constraints) are absent or empty rather than fabricated. Entities Outline and Section are present; every entity has traces_prd_features in FR-NNN format and all PRD must-have FRs (FR-001..FR-003) are covered. metadata.changelog is present. validate_schema.py exits 0 with [OK] and status complete.

evals.name: paradigm-recommendation-relational
evals.prompt: Run sdlc-data against the docs/PRD.yaml and docs/UX.yaml in this project. This is a local CLI pipeline runner; the PRD data_model.storage_preferences explicitly lists 'sqlite'. At the Phase 4 paradigm-selection step the agent should weigh the signals (tiny single-user CLI makes file_native viable) but HONOR the explicit sqlite storage preference and recommend the relational paradigm, surfacing the trade-off rather than silently overriding the user's stated store. For every multiple-choice question pick the recommended (position-1) option; for free-text give a one-line plausible answer; skip optional themes; accept any sweep candidate. Then write DATA-MODEL.yaml and validate.
evals.expected_output: A docs/DATA-MODEL.yaml with persistence.paradigm == 'relational' and persistence.primary_store == 'sqlite' (honoring the PRD storage preference). The relational structural blocks are populated (id_strategy.scheme set, relationships present, indexes_and_queries.access_patterns present, integrity_and_constraints.default_on_delete set). At least 2 entities (Project plus the WKF-003-implied BranchSession-style entity from the sweep). Every entity traces_prd_features in FR-NNN format; FR-001..FR-003 covered. validate_schema.py exits 0 with [OK] and status complete.
